scenario: loss of confidence after a failed creative project

turns.user_message: I used to think of myself as a creative person but after my last project fell apart, I don't know who I am anymore.
Yeah... I guess what I mean is, this thing with a failed creative project is never going to get better.
I tried to talk about this with my therapist friend but they just said I should just get over it and move on.
Yeah. And what makes it harder is that nobody around me seems to understand.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by a failed creative project more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with a failed creative project and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting a failed creative project control my life more than I realized.

I just realized something. the a failed creative project is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
